description: Trust configuration for SkillGuard. Internal skills get vulnerability-focused scanning (real bugs, hardcoded secrets, reverse shells). External skills get full threat-model scanning (intent analysis, behavioral signatures).

_notes.prompt-injection: Deliberately omitted from alwaysFlagCategories — context-dependent. A security doc describing injection patterns isn't the same as actual injection. The code's applyTrustSuppression() handles PI with nuanced checks (SKILL.md, references/, tests, detection code, etc.).

notes: Trusted skills still get scanned — we want to catch actual vulnerabilities in our own code. What we suppress is 'this code uses exec/fetch/env' which is expected for infrastructure tools we authored.
